heading: Definitions and special rules
label: 4132 Definitions and special rules

Effective Date of 2013 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–15, § 1(b)127 Stat. 476June 25, 2013“(1).—Sales, etcThe amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to sales and uses on or after the later of—“(A)the first day of the first month which begins more than 4 weeks after the date of the enactment of this Act [], orJune 25, 2013“(B)the date on which the Secretary of Health and Human Services lists any vaccine against seasonal influenza (other than any vaccine against seasonal influenza listed by the Secretary prior to the date of the enactment of this Act) for purposes of compensation for any vaccine-related injury or death through the Vaccine Injury Compensation Trust Fund.“(2).—DeliveriesFor purposes of paragraph (1) and section 4131 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of sales on or before the effective date described in such paragraph for which delivery is made after such date, the delivery date shall be considered the sale date.”

children.heading: Definitions relating to taxable vaccines
children.label: a Definitions relating to taxable vaccines
A
children.text: Any vaccine containing diphtheria toxoid.

children.label: B
children.text: Any vaccine containing tetanus toxoid.

children.label: C
children.text: Any vaccine containing pertussis bacteria, extracted or partial cell bacteria, or specific pertussis antigens.

children.label: D
children.text: Any vaccine against measles.

children.label: E
children.text: Any vaccine against mumps.

children.label: F
children.text: Any vaccine against rubella.

children.label: G
children.text: Any vaccine containing polio virus.

children.label: H
children.text: Any HIB vaccine.

children.label: I
children.text: Any vaccine against hepatitis A.

children.label: J
children.text: Any vaccine against hepatitis B.

children.label: K
children.text: Any vaccine against chicken pox.

children.label: L
children.text: Any vaccine against rotavirus gastroenteritis.

children.label: M
children.text: Any conjugate vaccine against streptococcus pneumoniae.

children.label: N
children.text: Any trivalent vaccine against influenza or any other vaccine against seasonal influenza.

children.label: O
children.text: Any meningococcal vaccine.

children.label: P
children.text: Any vaccine against the human papillomavirus.

children.heading: Vaccine
children.label: 2 Vaccine
children.text: The term “vaccine” means any substance designed to be administered to a human being for the prevention of 1 or more diseases.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 3 United States
children.text: The term “United States” has the meaning given such term by section 4612(a)(4).

children.heading: Importer
children.label: 4 Importer
children.text: The term “importer” means the person entering the vaccine for consumption, use, or warehousing.

children.heading: Credit or refund where vaccine returned to manufacturer, etc., or destroyed
children.label: b Credit or refund where vaccine returned to manufacturer, etc., or destroyed
A
children.text: returned (other than for resale) to the person who paid such tax, or

children.label: B
children.text: destroyed,

children.heading: Claim must be filed within 6 months
children.label: 2 Claim must be filed within 6 months
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall apply to any returned or destroyed vaccine only with respect to claims filed within 6 months after the date the vaccine is returned or destroyed.

children.heading: Condition of allowance of credit or refund
children.label: 3 Condition of allowance of credit or refund
A
children.text: has repaid or agreed to repay the amount of the tax to the ultimate purchaser of the vaccine, or

children.label: B
children.text: has obtained the written consent of such purchaser to the allowance of the credit or the making of the refund.

children.heading: Tax imposed only once
children.label: 4 Tax imposed only once
children.text: No tax shall be imposed by section 4131 on the sale of any vaccine if tax was imposed by section 4131 on any prior sale of such vaccine and such tax is not abated, credited, or refunded.

children.heading: Certain uses treated as sales
children.label: 1 Certain uses treated as sales
children.text: Any manufacturer, producer, or importer of a vaccine which uses such vaccine before it is sold shall be liable for the tax imposed by section 4131 in the same manner as if such vaccine were sold by such manufacturer, producer, or importer.

children.heading: Treatment of vaccines shipped to United States possessions
children.label: 2 Treatment of vaccines shipped to United States possessions
children.text: Section 4221(a)(2) shall not apply to any vaccine shipped to a possession of the United States.

children.heading: Fractional part of a dose
children.label: 3 Fractional part of a dose
children.text: In the case of a fraction of a dose, the tax imposed by section 4131 shall be the same fraction of the amount of such tax imposed by a whole dose.

children.heading: Disposition of revenues from Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands
children.label: 4 Disposition of revenues from Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands
children.text: The provisions of subsections (a)(3) and (b)(3) of section 7652 shall not apply to any tax imposed by section 4131.
